Peaceful protests over George Floyd killing continue in Portland
Thousands of protesters march over the Burnside Bridge Tuesday night, June 2, 2020 over the killing of George Floyd by police in Minneapolis. (KATU/Chopper 2)

image_captions: Thousands of protesters march over the Burnside Bridge Tuesday night, June 2, 2020 over the killing of George Floyd by police in Minneapolis. (KATU/Chopper 2)
Protest over death of George Floyd in Portland on June 2, 2020 - KATU image.
Photo of the group of protesters at SE 13th and Stark on June 2, 2020 - Image from Megan Allison.

evidence.title: Portland's Leading Local News: Weather, Traffic, Sports and more | Portland, Oregon | KGW.com
evidence.description: The group started at a rally in Laurelhurst Park, then made their way to E Burnside to cross the bridge into downtown.

evidence.page_title: Burnside Bridge in Portland marks 100 years with family-friendly party
evidence.title: Burnside Bridge in Portland marks 100 years with family-friendly party | Haystack News
evidence.description: Burnside Bridge in Portland marks 100 years with family-friendly party
evidence.text:
FAQ
How does Haystack News work?
Haystack News makes it easy to see the news that matters to you. Tell us the sources, topics and places you care about and we'll create a news channel made just for you called "My Headlines" from more than 400 different trusted channels. You can also take a deep dive into topics like weather, entertainment, international news in the channel guide and watch live news from local, national and global channels.
Is Haystack News really free?
Yes! Haystack provides free access, supported by ads, making news widely accessible without subscription fees. If you prefer not to see ads, you can upgrade to our Premium tier.
Where can I watch Haystack News?
Haystack is available on mobile devices, smart TVs, streaming devices, and the web. Can't find Haystack on your device? Let us know at feedback@haystack.tv.
How can I customize my news based on my interests?
How can I skip ads?
Upgrade to Premium. For just $4.99 per month, you can get all your news and no commercials. You can try it for free for 30 days!
Why are you called Haystack News?
Haystack News was born in 2015 because it was hard to stream high-quality, trusted news without sifting through masses of irrelevant video. It's our goal to get you the info you want and needâthe news equivalent of the proverbial needle in a haystackâwithout wasting your time on things that don't matter to you. Have another question? Check out our full FAQ.
